content.text:
Data Analysis
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEM1  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental concepts of data analysis
Develop skills in data exploration, cleaning and transformation
Understand and apply different statistical and predictive analysis techniques
Use data analysis and visualisation tools
Interpret analysis results and communicate them effectively
Recommended prior knowledge:
Algebra, descriptive statistics, and probability.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Data Analysis
Review: linear algebra, probability, and descriptive statistics
Definition and challenges of data analysis in the context of information systems engineering
Data types (structured, semi-structured, unstructured)
Stages of the data analysis life cycle
Data Exploration and Preparation
Data exploration techniques (visualisation, descriptive statistics)
Data cleaning (handling missing values, error detection and correction)
Data transformation (normalisation, aggregation, new variable creation)
Descriptive and Inferential Statistical Analysis
Descriptive statistics (mean, variance, correlation, etc.)
Hypothesis testing (t-tests, ANOVA, chi-square)
Linear and logistic regression
Predictive Analysis Techniques
Decision trees and random forests
Support vector machines
Neural networks
Time series models
Classification Methods
Supervised and unsupervised classification
Partitioning methods
Hierarchical ascending classification
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): TP tests, personal work
Practical work:
Use of Python; exploration of common libraries (pandas, TensorFlow, etc.) and visualisation tools (matplotlib, seaborn, tableau, etc.).
References
Petrelli, M. (2021).
Introduction to Python in Earth Science Data Analysis
. Springer Nature.
Provost, F., & Fawcett, T. (2013).
Data Science for Business
. O'Reilly Media.
DasGupta, A. (2011).
Probability for statistics and machine learning
. Springer.
Montgomery, D. C., & Runger, G. C. (2010).
Applied Statistics and Probability for Engineers
. John Wiley & Sons.
VanderPlas, J. (2016).
Python Data Science Handbook
. O'Reilly Media.
Brigitte Escofier, Jérôme Pagès (2016).
Analyses factorielles simples et multiples
. 5e édition. DUNOD.
